turns.notes: TRANSCRIPT TRUNCATES at 49:18 mid-decision with Alexandre lining up the winning play. Caster: 'It's going to be a double prize knockout thanks to the boss's orders. … And then their next turn boss's orders or if you really honestly you can just monkey dory and then [TRUNCATED]'

key_moments.description: Alexandre used Boss's Orders to gust Team Rocket's Mewtwo ex from bench BEFORE attacking with Phantom Dive — preempted the biggest attacker rather than killing the active. The Mewtwo had no bench-energy to discard (Spidops Charging Up hadn't fired yet) so it could only Psydrive for 160, leaving it 1-shottable next turn.
key_moments.engine_lesson: Validates `bestGustTarget` heuristic in src/engine/ai.ts — but 'gust the biggest threat that hasn't yet powered up' is a stronger frame than current 'gust ramp engines'. Mewtwo ex isn't a ramp engine but is a strategic priority gust because removing it before it gets bench energy denies its scaling +60-per-discarded-energy. Suggests adding `gustPriorityForUnpoweredPunchers` to aiArchetype.ts.

key_moments.description: Alexandre spread Crispin energies across multiple Drakloak rather than committing both to one — explicitly to defuse Team Rocket's Giovanni gust threat. Caster: 'spread the energy over multiple Pokemon rather than committing to just one'.
key_moments.engine_lesson: src/engine/ai.ts `scorePosition` does NOT currently model 'gust-defusal via redundant energy attachers'. The threat-aware OHKO penalty only looks at the active. Add: when opp has Boss's-equivalent supporter known/likely, reward distributing energy across redundant attackers.

key_moments.description: Mateesh chained Team Rocket's Transceiver → Team Rocket's Petrel → Maximum Belt (ACE SPEC) onto Mewtwo, then Spidops Charging Up x2 from discard, then Psydrive boosted to 330 (160 + 120 from 2 discarded bench energies + 50 Maximum Belt) for the GAME-WINNING KO on Blaziken ex.
key_moments.engine_lesson: This is THE canonical TR Mewtwo finishing combo. Encode in aiArchetype.ts as the 'TR Mewtwo Petrel-into-ACE-SPEC line' once the engine has a Rocket Mewtwo archetype detector. Trainer-effects.ts already has `arianaDrawUntilTR` and `protonSearchBasicTR`; would need a `petrelSearchAceSpec` trainer effect to mirror this in-engine.

key_moments.description: Mateesh PASSED on a 280-damage Mewtwo Psydrive into a 280-HP Dragapult ex with no Articuno cover — caster framed it as 'fear of Adrena-Brain' (Munkidori counter-play) but called it the 'intriguing pass' that 'changed the tide of this game'. The decision allowed Dragapult to survive and counter-KO with Phantom Dive next turn.
key_moments.engine_lesson: AI v2 should AGGRESSIVELY take 2-prize KOs when they don't expose to immediate counter-KO. Current `scorePosition` rewards prize differential heavily, but the lookahead minimax in ai.ts may currently over-weight 'opp can survive and Adrena-Brain' if Munkidori is on board. Consider a tunable `passOnLetalConservatismFactor` — should default LOW (real human pros also consider this play questionable). This is a perfect Phase 9 self-tuning candidate.

key_moments.description: Alexandre (Dragapult-Blaziken, the slower archetype) deliberately CHOSE to go first to deny Mateesh's T1 Team Rocket's Proton — accepting the Phantom Dive T2 ban in exchange for delaying opp's Proton search by one turn.
key_moments.engine_lesson: The engine's `T1_SUPPORTER_EXCEPTIONS` already encodes that Proton works on T1 going first. AI v2's choose-first/second logic in ai.ts should consider 'opp's archetype's T1 supporter exception is strong enough to deny by going first' — currently opening-pick is purely 'maximize own value of T1 supporter'. Add: subtract opp's expected T1-going-first value from going-second value when comparing.

engine_notes: TR Mewtwo T1-going-first opener is highly canonical: PROTON → fetch 3 TR Basics to HAND (not bench, important — preserves Ariana scaling) → bench 1 basic + Transceiver-tutor an Ariana for next-turn supporter → Ultra Ball → manual TR Energy attach. Add to data/tournament-replays/opening-book.json as 'rocket-mewtwo' / going_first: true.

Dragapult-Blaziken T2-going-second opener (this match's game 1): Lillie's Determination + Buddy-Buddy Poffin to flood Torchic + 2-3 Dreepy → Budew active → Itchy Pollen for item lock. Strong candidate for the engine's archetype playbook — note this is a STALL-FIRST opener, not a Phantom-Dive-rush opener. The engine's `aiArchetype.ts` has 'Festival Lead' but no 'Stall + Stage 2 Setup' archetype.

Both decklists rely on supporter chains: Petrel→ACE-SPEC tutor (TR Mewtwo) and Crispin→energy-search (Dragapult-Blaziken). The engine's `trainerEffects.ts` has Crispin already; would need to add Team Rocket's Petrel as a tutor for ACE SPEC trainer (single-target trainer search restricted to ACE SPEC subtype).

Munkidori 'Adrena-Brain' is decisive as both a defensive (heal own) AND an offensive (snipe-finish) tool. abilities.ts already has `moveDamageOwnToOpp` listed (interactive). Real game showed it being used for Phantom Dive math twice in a single turn — verify the engine permits multiple uses/turn (it should: ability used per Munkidori instance).

Mewtwo ex's bench-energy-discard scaling is the central reason TR Mewtwo wants 2+ Spidops fully charged. Engine damage estimator needs to evaluate 'energies on bench Pokémon' for Mewtwo ex's attack — confirm executeAttackHit picks this up.

Strong evidence that 'spread energy across multiple attackers to defuse gust' is real human pro play that the AI doesn't currently learn. Add a heuristic in scorePosition to reward N_ready_attackers when opp has Boss's/Giovanni-equivalent supporters known.

Mimikyu copy-attack mechanic was decisive — caster repeatedly noted 'Mimikyu can copy Phantom Dive' to win mirrors. Engine's `effects.ts` has copy-attack pipelines already; verify TR Mimikyu's restriction (Terror-Pokémon attacks only) is properly scoped.

Game 3 demonstrated the cost of prized engine pieces: 4/7 basic energies + both Rare Candy prized. The engine's mulligan/setup currently doesn't surface 'prize-card composition risk' — minor, but worth noting that pro players often play through bad prizes by leaning on Crispin/Night Stretcher fetch. Crispin and Night Stretcher are both already in trainerEffects.ts.
